name: OPEC Statute 1960 (Organization of the Petroleum Exporting Countries)
summary: The Statute of the Organization of the Petroleum Exporting Countries (OPEC Statute) is a global supranational meta-framework foundational-OPEC-treaty framework adopted by the 5 founding petroleum-exporting states in Baghdad — structurally distinctive globally as the only modern global supranational binding-treaty framework operating on formal petroleum-exporting-states framework + global oil-price-management cartel framework (OPEC Statute adopted 14 September 1960 at Baghdad Conference in Baghdad Iraq by Iran + Iraq + Kuwait + Saudi Arabia + Venezuela + amended multiple times most recently 2012 — formal foundational OPEC treaty framework establishing comprehensive OPEC organisation framework, distinct from rest-of-modern-international-treaty frameworks which operate without commodity-specific framework, structurally distinctive globally as the only modern global supranational binding-treaty framework operating on formal petroleum-exporting-states framework + global oil-price-management cartel framework + only modern global supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal production-quota framework + only modern global supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal OPEC-Plus framework with Russia + Mexico + Kazakhstan + Oman + 6 other non-OPEC oil-producers framework from 2016 + only modern global supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal Qatar-departure-2019 + Ecuador-departure-2020 + Indonesia-departure-2016 + Angola-departure-2024 framework + only modern global supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal ~40% global oil production framework + ~80% proven oil reserves framework), as the central jurisdiction of the formal OPEC Production Quota framework (OPEC Production Quota framework providing formal production-quota allocation framework via OPEC Ministerial Meeting + OPEC Conference framework — formal foundational OPEC Production Quota framework, structurally distinctive globally as the only modern global supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal production-quota framework + only modern global supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal coordinated-supply-management framework), as the central jurisdiction of the formal OPEC-Plus 2016 framework (OPEC-Plus framework established 10 December 2016 in Vienna Austria + Declaration of Cooperation DoC signed providing formal expansion-cooperation framework with Russia + Mexico + Kazakhstan + Oman + Azerbaijan + Bahrain + Brunei + Malaysia + Sudan + South Sudan non-OPEC oil-producers framework — formal foundational OPEC-Plus framework, structurally distinctive globally as the only modern global supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal OPEC-Plus framework + only modern global supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal Russia-Saudi-Arabia-oil-cooperation framework), and as the central jurisdiction of the formal OPEC Member Departures 2016-2024 framework (OPEC member departures via Indonesia withdrew 30 November 2016 + Qatar withdrew 1 January 2019 + Ecuador withdrew 1 January 2020 + Angola withdrew 1 January 2024 reducing OPEC from 13 members 2018 peak to 12 active members 2026 framework — formal foundational OPEC member-departures framework, structurally distinctive globally as the only modern global supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal four-member-departures-in-eight-years framework). The OPEC Statute is operating upon 12 OPEC member states as of 2026 (Algeria + Congo + Equatorial Guinea + Gabon + Iran + Iraq + Kuwait + Libya + Nigeria + Saudi Arabia + UAE + Venezuela) + 10 non-OPEC partners via OPEC-Plus framework — comprehensive petroleum-exporting-states coverage framework. The Statute is silent on 'parental alienation' as a statutory label, though formal OPEC framework operates limited intersection with parental-alienation framework via formal petroleum-exporting-states + Gulf-Arab-family-law framework not directly addressing family-law-issues framework. The OPEC Conference + OPEC Board of Governors + OPEC Secretary General + OPEC Secretariat + OPEC-Plus Joint Ministerial Monitoring Committee JMMC are the formal OPEC bodies; national-level apex courts operate as judicial bodies. The OPEC Statute is silent on 'parental alienation' as a statutory label.

statutory_framework.title: OPEC Statute
statutory_framework.relevance: Statute of the Organization of the Petroleum Exporting Countries adopted 14 September 1960 at Baghdad Conference in Baghdad Iraq by Iran + Iraq + Kuwait + Saudi Arabia + Venezuela — formal foundational OPEC treaty framework establishing comprehensive OPEC organisation framework.

statutory_framework.title: OPEC Statute Article 3
statutory_framework.relevance: OPEC Statute Article 3 framework on OPEC objectives: coordination + unification of petroleum policies + securing fair + stable prices for petroleum producers framework — formal foundational OPEC objectives framework.

statutory_framework.citation: OPEC Declaratory Statement of Petroleum Policy in Member Countries 1968
statutory_framework.title: OPEC Declaratory Statement of Petroleum Policy
statutory_framework.relevance: OPEC Declaratory Statement of Petroleum Policy in Member Countries adopted 24-25 June 1968 — formal foundational OPEC petroleum-policy framework.

statutory_framework.title: OPEC Long-Term Strategy
statutory_framework.relevance: OPEC Long-Term Strategy adopted 1978 — formal foundational OPEC long-term-strategy framework.

statutory_framework.title: OPEC-Plus DoC
statutory_framework.relevance: OPEC-Plus Declaration of Cooperation DoC signed 10 December 2016 in Vienna Austria — formal foundational OPEC-Plus framework.

apex_courts.name: OPEC Conference
apex_courts.role: Formal supreme decision-making body for OPEC.

apex_courts.name: OPEC Secretary General
apex_courts.role: Formal administrative head body for OPEC.

anonymisation_convention: OPEC-related communications operate via OPEC Conference + OPEC Board of Governors framework; individual communications not formally accepted under OPEC framework.

key_developments.title: OPEC Statute adopted
key_developments.description: OPEC Statute adopted 14 September 1960 at Baghdad Conference in Baghdad Iraq by Iran + Iraq + Kuwait + Saudi Arabia + Venezuela — formal foundational OPEC treaty framework adopted.

key_developments.title: OPEC Headquarters moves to Vienna
key_developments.description: OPEC Headquarters moved from Geneva Switzerland to Vienna Austria 1 September 1965 — formal OPEC Headquarters framework operational.

key_developments.title: 1973 Oil Crisis + Arab Oil Embargo
key_developments.description: 1973 Oil Crisis + Arab Oil Embargo 17 October 1973 - 17 March 1974 imposed by OAPEC during Yom Kippur War + OPEC quadrupling oil prices — formal foundational OPEC oil-pricing-power framework operational.

key_developments.title: Iranian Revolution + 1979 Oil Crisis
key_developments.description: Iranian Revolution 1979 + 1979 Oil Crisis — formal OPEC oil-pricing framework operational.

key_developments.title: Ecuador withdraws + rejoins 2007
key_developments.description: Ecuador withdrew from OPEC 1992 + rejoined 2007 — formal OPEC member-withdrawal-rejoining framework.

key_developments.title: Gabon withdraws + rejoins 2016
key_developments.description: Gabon withdrew from OPEC 1995 + rejoined 1 July 2016 — formal OPEC member-withdrawal-rejoining framework.

key_developments.title: OPEC-Plus established + Indonesia withdraws
key_developments.description: OPEC-Plus Declaration of Cooperation DoC signed 10 December 2016 in Vienna Austria + Indonesia withdrew from OPEC 30 November 2016 — formal foundational OPEC-Plus + member-departure framework operational.

key_developments.title: Qatar withdraws from OPEC
key_developments.description: Qatar withdrew from OPEC 1 January 2019 — formal OPEC member-departure framework operational.

key_developments.title: Ecuador withdraws + COVID-19 oil price war
key_developments.description: Ecuador withdrew from OPEC 1 January 2020 + COVID-19 oil price war March-April 2020 between Saudi Arabia + Russia + historic OPEC-Plus cut 9.7 million barrels per day from 12 April 2020 — formal OPEC member-departure + COVID-19-oil-crisis framework operational.

key_developments.title: Angola withdraws from OPEC
key_developments.description: Angola withdrew from OPEC 1 January 2024 — formal OPEC member-departure framework operational reducing OPEC to 12 members.

structural_findings: OPEC Statute operates a supranational global binding-treaty framework — places OPEC Statute in the global supranational-meta-framework cluster.

Only modern global supranational binding-treaty framework operating on formal petroleum-exporting-states framework + global oil-price-management cartel framework is structurally distinctive globally — only modern global supranational binding-treaty framework operating on formal petroleum-exporting-states framework + global oil-price-management cartel framework + only modern global supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal production-quota framework + only modern global supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal OPEC-Plus framework with Russia + Mexico + Kazakhstan + Oman + 6 other non-OPEC oil-producers framework from 2016 + only modern global supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal Qatar-departure-2019 + Ecuador-departure-2020 + Indonesia-departure-2016 + Angola-departure-2024 framework + only modern global supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal ~40% global oil production framework + ~80% proven oil reserves framework.

Central jurisdiction of formal OPEC Production Quota framework is structurally distinctive globally — only modern global supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal production-quota framework + only modern global supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal coordinated-supply-management framework.

Central jurisdiction of formal OPEC-Plus 2016 framework is structurally distinctive globally — only modern global supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal OPEC-Plus framework + only modern global supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal Russia-Saudi-Arabia-oil-cooperation framework.

Central jurisdiction of formal OPEC Member Departures 2016-2024 framework is structurally distinctive globally — only modern global supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal four-member-departures-in-eight-years framework.
12 OPEC member state + 10 non-OPEC partner ratification framework is structurally distinctive globally — comprehensive petroleum-exporting-states coverage framework.

1960 OPEC Statute + 1965 Vienna Headquarters + 1973 1973 Oil Crisis + Arab Oil Embargo + 1979 Iranian Revolution + 1992 Ecuador withdraws + 1995 Gabon withdraws + 2016 OPEC-Plus + Indonesia withdraws + 2019 Qatar withdraws + 2020 Ecuador withdraws + COVID-19 + 2024 Angola withdraws OPEC-framework-development sequence is structurally distinctive globally.

OPEC Statute + Declaratory Statement + Long-Term Strategy + OPEC-Plus DoC + Production Quota formal quintet framework with limited PA-intersection framework via formal petroleum-exporting-states + Gulf-Arab-family-law framework not directly addressing family-law-issues framework — distinctive in operating limited PA-intersection framework relative to other global supranational frameworks.
editorial_notes: OPEC Statute jurisdiction sidecar — supranational global petroleum-exporting-states foundational-OPEC-treaty meta-framework (OPEC Statute 1960 Baghdad + Article 3 Objectives + Declaratory Statement of Petroleum Policy 1968 + Long-Term Strategy 1978 + OPEC-Plus DoC 2016 Vienna + Indonesia 2016 + Qatar 2019 + Ecuador 2020 + Angola 2024 withdrawals + 1973 Oil Crisis + Arab Oil Embargo + 1979 Iranian Revolution + COVID-19 2020 historic 9.7 million bpd cut). Only modern global supranational binding-treaty framework operating on formal petroleum-exporting-states framework + global oil-price-management cartel framework globally + ~40% global oil production + ~80% proven oil reserves + only modern global supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal production-quota framework + only modern global supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal OPEC-Plus framework + only modern global supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal four-member-departures-in-eight-years framework + central jurisdiction of formal OPEC Production Quota + central jurisdiction of formal OPEC-Plus + central jurisdiction of formal Member Departures 2016-2024 framework + 12 OPEC member states (Algeria + Congo + Equatorial Guinea + Gabon + Iran + Iraq + Kuwait + Libya + Nigeria + Saudi Arabia + UAE + Venezuela) + 10 non-OPEC partners via OPEC-Plus + 1960 OPEC Statute + 1965 Vienna + 1973 Oil Crisis + Arab Oil Embargo + 1979 Iranian Revolution + 1992 Ecuador withdraws + 1995 Gabon withdraws + 2016 OPEC-Plus + Indonesia + 2019 Qatar + 2020 Ecuador + COVID-19 + 2024 Angola OPEC-framework-development sequence.

PA-recognition: silent statutory + no-apex-position + silent regulator. NOTE: OPEC framework operates limited intersection with parental-alienation framework via formal petroleum-exporting-states + Gulf-Arab-family-law framework not directly addressing family-law-issues framework — distinctive in operating limited PA-intersection framework relative to other global supranational frameworks.
